### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request. **Reviewed commit:** `f607bb63ba`
ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

## Pull request overview

Adds a new “dogfood proof” bundle for the 2026-05-27 run7 execution under `proof/dogfood/2026-05-27/run7/`, capturing offline guard behavior (budget/loop/retry enforcement), installed-vs-checkout CLI behavior, and a repo health/release snapshot to support ongoing SDK validation without touching dashboard/control-plane code.

**Changes:**
- Adds run7 traces (`*.jsonl`) and derived summaries/reports (`*.md`, `*.log`, `*.json`) for checkout and temporary-venv installs.
- Captures failure evidence for the globally-installed CLI due to stale user-site editable metadata.
- Records repo state snapshots (open PRs/issues, release/distribution lookups, review-thread summaries).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 27 out of 44 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Regenerate the corrupted review-loop log**

For anyone using this dogfood run as proof, `review-loop.log` is not a clean command transcript: it starts with mojibake instead of the checkout-review heading and also has a corrupted exit marker at the end. Since this PR is adding proof artifacts, keeping an encoding-corrupted log makes the evidence hard to audit with normal text tooling and undermines the run summary; please regenerate or re-encode this log as plain UTF-8 before committing it.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

`Estimated cost: $0.1020` is inconsistent with the underlying `review_loop_trace.jsonl`, which contains three `review.iteration` events with top-level `cost_usd` values summing to $0.0510 (0.0135 + 0.0170 + 0.0205). For proof artifacts, please either regenerate this report with the corrected cost accounting or add an explicit note in the report that this value is known to be double-counted on `main` so readers don’t treat it as ground truth.

This incident report lists `Estimated cost: $0.1020`, but the associated `review_loop_trace.jsonl` has three `review.iteration` events whose `cost_usd` values sum to $0.0510. Please regenerate the incident report after the cost double-counting fix, or add an explicit “known issue” note so the incident summary doesn’t mislead readers about actual spend.
